transliteration: tatrāpaśyat sthitān pārthaḥ pitṝn atha pitāmahān ācāryān mātulān bhrātṝn putrān pautrān sakhīṃs tathā śvaśurān suhṛdaś caiva senayor ubhayor api
literal_meaning: There Arjuna saw stationed, O Partha, fathers, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, friends, fathers-in-law, and also well-wishers in both armies.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s panoramic vision collapses the abstract idea of war into an unbearably intimate tableau: every significant attachment figure from his life stares back at him across the dust of Kurukshetra. The verse marks the instant when political conflict becomes a mirror of inner fragmentation, forcing him to confront the impossibility of violence without self-violence.
psychological_state: The moment when every meaningful relationship is simultaneously perceived as both sacred and endangered, freezing the capacity for action.
trigger_scenario: A team-lead realizes the upcoming corporate layoff will terminate lifelong mentors, close friends, and even their own child’s internship, making any decision feel like self-amputation.
anchor_text: When every face is your own, war becomes suicide.
